<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);

    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);

    target->notify();

    return std::make_pair(observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount());
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test thread manager registration and safety check
 *
 * @param enableSafety Whether to enable thread safety
 * @return bool Whether thread safety is enabled after operations
 */
bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    reaction::ThreadManager& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    if (enableSafety) {
        tm.enableThreadSafety(enableSafety);
    }
    return tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition
 */
int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    auto var = reaction::VarExpr<int>::create(initialValue);
    var->atomicAddAssign(addValue);
    return var->get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_calculation(int a, int b) {
    auto varA = reaction::createVar(a, "varA");
    auto varB = reaction::createVar(b, "varB");
    auto calc = reaction::createCalcExpr(
        [](int x, int y) { return (x + y) * 2; },
        {varA, varB}
    );
    return calc->getPtr()->getValue();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return true if all batch operations succeed, false otherwise
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    // Step 1: Create observer nodes
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();

    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 3: Create batch ID
    auto batch = graph.createBatch();

    // Step 4: Register nodes to batch
    batch->addNode(node1);
    batch->addNode(node2);

    // Step 5: Verify nodes are in batch
    auto activeNodes = graph.getActiveBatchNodes();
    if (activeNodes.size() != 2 || 
        !activeNodes.count(node1) || 
        !activeNodes.count(node2)) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 6: Unregister batch
    batch->close();

    // Step 7: Verify nodes are no longer in batch
    activeNodes = graph.getActiveBatchNodes();
    if (!activeNodes.empty()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 8: Return success status
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================
class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() { // Added reset method
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================
bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Step 1: Create observer node
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);

    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 3: Create source node and add observer relationship
    auto source = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    graph.addObserver(source, observer);

    // Step 4: Notify source and verify observer was notified
    source->notify();
    if (!observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 5: Reset observer state
    observer->reset();

    // Step 6: Close observer node
    graph.closeObserver(observer);

    // Step 7: Notify source again
    source->notify();

    // Step 8: Verify observer was not notified after closure
    if (observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 0) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <stdexcept>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test transactional observer updates
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the node to update
 * @return bool Whether transactional update succeeded (no cycle created)
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Step 1: Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("source");
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");

    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    ObserverGraph& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Register nodes to the graph
    graph.registerNode(source);
    graph.registerNode(target);

    // Step 3: Set up initial relationship (source observes target)
    graph.addObserver(source, target);

    // Step 4: Notify target and verify relationship works
    source->valueChanged();
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 5: Reset observers for next test
    source->reset();
    target->reset();

    // Step 6: Attempt transactional update that would create cycle
    try {
        // Transactional update: Add target observes source (creates cycle)
        graph.addObserverTransactional(target, source);
        // If this succeeds, it's a failure case
        return false;
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        // Cycle detected, expected exception
    }

    // Step 8: Notify target again
    source->valueChanged();

    // Step 9: Verify original relationship is preserved
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 10: Return success status
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {

    // Create target node

    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);

    // Create observer nodes

    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;

    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {

        auto observer = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name);

        observers.push_back(observer);

    }

    // Get observer graph instance

    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add all observers to watch target

    for (auto& observer : observers) {

        graph.addObserver(target, observer);

    }

    // Notify target

    target->notify();

    // Count notifications and verify all observers were notified

    int total = 0;

    bool allNotified = true;

    for (auto& observer : observers) {

        total += observer->getCallCount();

        if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {

            allNotified = false;

        }

    }

    return {total, allNotified};

}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        auto observer = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name);
        observers.push_back(observer);
    }
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add all observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    // Notify target
    target->notify();
    
    // Count notifications and verify all observers were notified
    int total = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        total += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    return {total, allNotified};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);

    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObserver(target, observer1);
    graph.addObserver(observer1, observer2);

    target->notify();

    int total = observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    bool chainStatus = (observer1->getCallCount() > 0) && (observer2->getCallCount() > 0);

    return {total, chainStatus};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 *

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

PersonRecord::PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
    : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

const std::string& PersonRecord::getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
int PersonRecord::getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

void PersonRecord::setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.set(name); }
void PersonRecord::setAge(int age) { m_age.set(age); }

/**
 * Function: Test reactive fields updating calculated info
 *
 * @param initialName Initial person name
 * @param initialAge Initial person age
 * @param newName Updated person name
 * @param newAge Updated person age
 * @return std::string Concatenated info after updates
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    
    // Create a reactive expression that combines name and age
    auto expr = reaction::make_calc_expr(
        [initialName = initialName, initialAge = initialAge](const std::string& name, int age) {
            return "Name: " + name + ", Age: " + std::to_string(age);
        },
        person.m_name, person.m_age
    );
    
    // Create a reactive variable to hold the calculated value
    reaction::Var<std::string> info(expr);
    
    // Update the fields
    person.setName(newName);
    person.setAge(newAge);
    
    // Return the updated info
    return info.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(
    int initialA,
    int initialB,
    int nonBatchA,
    int nonBatchB,
    int batchA,
    int batchB) {
    
    // Create reactive variables
    reaction::Var<int> a(initialA);
    reaction::Var<int> b(initialB);
    
    // Track recomputation count
    reaction::Var<int> recomputeCount(0);
    
    // Create calculation expression
    auto sumExpr = reaction::CalcExpr<reaction::AddOp, reaction::Var<int>, reaction::Var<int>>(a, b);
    
    // Create action to increment recompute count on sum change
    auto action = reaction::Action([this](const int& newValue, const int& oldValue) mutable {
        recomputeCount++;
    });
    sumExpr.addListener(action);
    
    // Perform non-batch updates
    a.set(nonBatchA);
    b.set(nonBatchB);
    int countAfterNonBatch = recomputeCount.get();
    
    // Execute batch updates
    {
        reaction::Batch batch([this](const reaction::NodePtr& node, const void* data) {
            a.set(batchA);
            b.set(batchB);
        });
        batch.execute();
    }
    int countAfterBatch = recomputeCount.get();
    
    return std::make_tuple(sumExpr.get(), countAfterNonBatch, countAfterBatch);
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // Create reactive nodes and variables
    auto sensorNode = std::make_shared<reaction::Node<double>>(initialValue);
    reaction::Var<double> sensorVar(sensorNode);

    reaction::Var<double> lastProcessedVar(initialValue);
    reaction::Var<int> recomputeCountVar(0);

    // Create filter-triggered calculation
    auto diffOp = reaction::AbsOp(sensorVar - lastProcessedVar);
    auto filterOp = reaction::GEOp(diffOp, diffThreshold);
    auto actionOp = reaction::AssignOp(lastProcessedVar, sensorVar) + reaction::IncrementOp(recomputeCountVar);
    reaction::Expr<reaction::FilterTrigOp<true>> filterTrigOpExpr = reaction::FilterTrigOp<true>(filterOp, actionOp);

    // Create node for the filter-triggered operation and add observer
    auto filterTrigNode = std::make_shared<reaction::Node<void>>();
    filterTrigNode->setOp(filterTrigOpExpr);
    filterTrigNode->addObCb(sensorVar.getPtr());

    // Update sensor with first value
    sensorVar.set(firstUpdate);

    // Record recompute count after first update
    int firstCount = recomputeCountVar.get();

    // Update sensor with second value
    sensorVar.set(secondUpdate);

    // Record recompute count after second update
    int secondCount = recomputeCountVar.get();

    // Determine final status
    std::string status = lastProcessedVar.get() >= levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";

    return std::make_tuple(status, firstCount, secondCount);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged
inline void FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) {
    if (changed) {
        m_notificationCount++;
    }
}

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode::getNotificationCount
inline int FieldOwnerNode::getNotificationCount() const {
    return m_notificationCount;
}

// Implementation of ProfileFields constructor
inline ProfileFields::ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
    : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}

// Implementation of ProfileFields::score and active accessors
inline reaction::Var<int>& ProfileFields::score() { return m_score; }
inline reaction::Var<bool>& ProfileFields::active() { return m_active; }

// Implementation of test_field_binding
inline FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    auto profile = std::make_shared<ProfileFields>(initialScore, true);
    auto owner = std::make_shared<FieldOwnerNode>();

    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(owner);

    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.bindField(profile->score(), owner);
    fieldGraph.bindField(profile->active(), owner);

    // Update score and record notifications
    profile->score().set(initialScore + scoreDelta);
    int notificationsAfterScore = owner->getNotificationCount();

    // Update active and record notifications
    profile->active().set(!profile->active().get());
    int notificationsAfterStatus = owner->getNotificationCount();

    return FieldBindingResult{
        .notificationsAfterScoreUpdate = notificationsAfterScore,
        .notificationsAfterStatusUpdate = notificationsAfterStatus};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of RecordingNode methods
inline void RecordingNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) {
    ++m_notifications;
}
inline int RecordingNode::notificationCount() const {
    return m_notifications;
}

// Implementation of test_observer_state_rollback
inline test_reaction::RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto node3 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();

    ObserverGraph& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(node1);
    graph.registerNode(node2);
    graph.registerNode(node3);

    graph.addObserver(node2, node1);

    // Save current state for rollback
    auto rollback = graph.saveStateForRollback();

    // Replace dependencies transactionally
    graph.removeObserver(node2, node1);
    graph.addObserver(node2, node3);

    // Trigger notification on second sensor (node3)
    node3->valueChanged();
    int notificationsAfterSecondBinding = node2->notificationCount();

    // Execute rollback
    rollback();

    // Trigger notification on first sensor (node1)
    node1->valueChanged();
    int notificationsAfterRollback = node2->notificationCount();

    return RollbackObservation{
        .notificationsAfterSecondBinding = notificationsAfterSecondBinding,
        .notificationsAfterRollback = notificationsAfterRollback
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {
    int initialExpressionValue;
    int updatedExpressionValue;
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;
};

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variable
    auto var = reaction::createVar(baseValue);
    
    // Step 2: Create reactive expression (var + increment)
    auto expr = reaction::createExpr([increment = increment](const reaction::Var<int>& v) {
        return v.get() + increment;
    }, var);
    
    // Step 3: Create reactive calculation (var squared)
    auto calc = reaction::createCalc([](const reaction::Var<int>& v) {
        return v.get() * v.get();
    }, var);
    
    // Step 4: Get initial expression value
    int initialExprValue = expr.get();
    
    // Step 5: Update reactive variable
    var.set(baseValue + increment);
    
    // Step 6: Get updated expression value
    int updatedExprValue = expr.get();
    
    // Step 7: Get squared value after update
    int squaredValue = calc.get();
    
    // Step 8: Return results
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        .initialExpressionValue = initialExprValue,
        .updatedExpressionValue = updatedExprValue,
        .squaredValueAfterUpdate = squaredValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    // Create nodes
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("A");
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("B");
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("C");

    // Get graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Register nodes
    graph.registerNode(nodeA);
    graph.registerNode(nodeB);
    graph.registerNode(nodeC);

    // Set up direct observer relationship (A observes B)
    graph.addObserver(nodeA, nodeB, true);

    // Set up transitive observer relationship (B observes C)
    graph.addObserver(nodeB, nodeC, true);

    // Create NodeSet to collect observers
    reaction::NodeSet observersSet;

    // Collect observers from A
    graph.collectObservers(nodeA, observersSet);

    // Check if direct observer (B) is present
    bool hasDirect = observersSet.contains(nodeB);

    // Check if transitive observer (C) is present
    bool hasTransitive = observersSet.contains(nodeC);

    // Return summary
    return ObserverCollectionSummary{
        .totalObservers = observersSet.size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = hasDirect,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = hasTransitive
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyStatus {
    bool safetyBefore;
    bool safetyAfter;
    std::size_t threadCountBefore;
    std::size_t threadCountAfter;
};

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();
    threadManager.registerThread();
    
    auto beforeCount = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    auto beforeSafety = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    std::thread worker([]() {
        reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().registerThread();
    });
    worker.join();
    
    auto afterCount = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    auto afterSafety = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        .safetyBefore = beforeSafety,
        .safetyAfter = afterSafety,
        .threadCountBefore = beforeCount,
        .threadCountAfter = afterCount
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

void CacheProbeNode::valueChanged([[maybe_unused]] bool changed = true) {}

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeD = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();

    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(nodeA);
    graph.registerNode(nodeB);
    graph.registerNode(nodeC);
    graph.registerNode(nodeD);

    nodeA->addObCb(nodeB);
    nodeB->addObCb(nodeC);
    nodeC->addObCb(nodeD);

    NodeSet observers;
    graph.collectObservers(nodeA, observers);

    nodeB->changedNoNotify();
    nodeC->changedNoNotify();
    nodeB->valueChanged();
    nodeC->valueChanged();

    const auto& statsBefore = graph.getStats();
    size_t graphEntriesBefore = statsBefore.entries;

    graph.cleanup();

    const auto& statsAfter = graph.getStats();
    size_t graphEntriesAfter = statsAfter.entries;
    double hitRatioAfter = statsAfter.hitRatio;

    graph.closeNode(nodeA);
    graph.closeNode(nodeB);
    graph.closeNode(nodeC);
    graph.closeNode(nodeD);

    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        .graphEntriesBeforeCleanup = graphEntriesBefore,
        .graphEntriesAfterCleanup = graphEntriesAfter,
        .graphHitRatioAfterCleanup = hitRatioAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>
#include <mutex>
#include <shared_mutex>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    reaction::ThreadManager& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    tm.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    auto threadCount = tm.getThreadCount();
    bool beforeEnable = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    tm.enableThreadSafety();
    bool afterEnable = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    tm.reset();
    bool afterReset = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    return ThreadSafetyTransition{
        .beforeEnable = beforeEnable,
        .afterEnable = afterEnable,
        .afterReset = afterReset,
        .threadCount = threadCount
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

    dummy = calc.value();

    return AlwaysTriggerResult{
        .recomputeCountAfterInit = recomputeCountAfterInit,
        .recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <functional>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    bool safetyBeforeGuard = threadManager.getSafetyStatus();
    size_t threadCountBeforeGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    std::thread worker([this]() {
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard g;
        // Simulate work to ensure thread execution
        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(10));
    });

    worker.join();

    bool safetyAfterGuard = threadManager.getSafetyStatus();
    size_t threadCountAfterGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    return ThreadGuardState{
        .safetyBeforeGuard = safetyBeforeGuard,
        .safetyAfterGuard = safetyAfterGuard,
        .threadCountBeforeGuard = threadCountBeforeGuard,
        .threadCountAfterGuard = threadCountAfterGuard
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {
    int initialValue;
    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;
};

template <typename Op, typename Type>
using CalcExpr = reaction::CalcExpr<Op, Type>;

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable node
    auto varNode = std::make_shared<reaction::VarExpr<int>>(initialValue);
    
    // Create a calculation node with LastHandle policy
    auto calcExpr = std::make_shared<CalcExpr<reaction::LastHandle, int>>();
    calcExpr->addObCb(varNode, [](int value) { return value; });
    auto calcNode = reaction::createNode(calcExpr);
    
    // 1. Get initial result
    int initialResult = calcNode->get();
    
    // 2. Update the source variable
    varNode->set(updatedValue);
    
    // 3. Get updated result
    int updatedResult = calcNode->get();
    
    // 4. Close the source node
    varNode->close();
    
    // 5. Get result after source close
    int postCloseResult = calcNode->get();
    
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initialResult,
        .afterUpdate = updatedResult,
        .afterSourceClose = postCloseResult
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/core/id_generator.h>
#include <test_reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

UniqueIdSequence test_unique_id_sequence() {
    reaction::UniqueID id1, id2, id3;
    uint64_t first = id1.value();
    uint64_t second = id2.value();
    uint64_t third = id3.value();
    return UniqueIdSequence{
        .firstValue = first,
        .secondValue = second,

        .strictlyIncreasing = (first < second) && (second < third)
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#include <reaction/core/resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourceAtomicResult {
    int finalValue;
    bool operationReportedChange;
};

ResourceAtomicResult test_resource_atomic(int initialValue, int delta) {
    reaction::Resource<int> res(initialValue);
    bool changed = res.atomicAdd(delta);
    int finalValue = res.getValue();
    return ResourceAtomicResult{finalValue, changed};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitorReport report;

    // 1. Disable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().initialize();

    // 4. Check if monitoring is enabled before guard
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = StackMonitor::getInstance().isMonitoringEnabled();

    // 5. Create StackGuard in a scope block
    {
        StackGuard<sizeof(std::function<void()>)> guard; // Use a reasonable size
        if (!guard) [[unlikely]] {
            report.guardAllocated = false;
            return report; // Exit early if guard allocation fails
        }
        report.guardAllocated = true;

        // 7. Get current usage during guard
        report.usageDuringGuard = StackMonitor::getInstance().getCurrentUsage();

        // 8. Get statistics
        const StackMonitorStats& stats = StackMonitor::getInstance().getStats();
        report.usagePercentage = stats.usagePercentage;
        report.nearLimitDuringGuard = stats.nearLimit;
    }

    // 11. Get current usage after guard
    report.usageAfterGuard = StackMonitor::getInstance().getCurrentUsage();

    // 12. Disable monitoring again
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // 13. Check if monitoring is enabled after disable
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = StackMonitor::getInstance().isMonitoringEnabled();

    return report;
}

} // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // 1. Create lambda function to generate large payload
    auto createLargePayload = [largeSeed = largeSeed](const void*) {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = largeSeed + i;
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // 2. Create small SBOResource with initial value
    SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallRes{initialSmall};

    // 3. Create large SBOResource using the lambda
    SBOResource<LargePayload> largeRes{createLargePayload, largeSeed};

    // 4. Update small resource and check if change was reported
    bool updateChanged = smallRes.update(updatedSmall);

    // 5. Retrieve storage information
    auto smallStorage = smallRes.getStorageInfo();
    auto largeStorage = largeRes.getStorageInfo();

    // 6. Compile results into status structure
    return SboStorageStatus{
        .updateReportedChange = updateChanged,
        .smallUsingSBO = smallStorage.using_sbo,
        .smallStackUsage = smallStorage.stack_usage,
        .largeInitialized = largeStorage.initialized,
        .largeUsingSBO = largeStorage.using_sbo};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/conditional_mutex.h>

namespace test_reaction {

ConditionalMutexResult test_conditional_mutex_behavior() {
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.reset();

    reaction::ConditionalMutex conditional_mutex;

    // Test without safety enabled
    bool tryLockWithoutSafety = false;
    {
        reaction::ConditionalLock<reaction::ConditionalMutex> lock(conditional_mutex, false);
        tryLockWithoutSafety = true;
    }

    // Enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();

    // Test with safety enabled
    bool tryLockWithSafety = false;
    {
        reaction::ConditionalLock<reaction::ConditionalMutex> lock(conditional_mutex, true);
        tryLockWithSafety = true;
    }

    return ConditionalMutexResult{tryLockWithoutSafety, tryLockWithSafety};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {

        // 7. Attempt to acquire unique lock without safety
        result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = mutex.tryUniqueNoSafety();
        // 8. Unlock unique lock if acquired
        if (result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }
    
    // 10. Enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 11. Attempt to acquire shared lock with safety
    result.trySharedWithSafety = mutex.tryShared();
    
    // 12. Unlock shared lock if acquired
    if (result.trySharedWithSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }

        // 15. Unlock unique lock if acquired
        if (result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }
    
    // 17. Return shared mutex result
    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/memory/sbo.h> // Assuming this is where the SBO-related functions are located
#include <memory_config.h>       // For AlwaysThreshold, ConditionalThreshold, MaxStackUsage

namespace test_reaction {

SboTraitSummary analyze_sbo_traits() {
    SboTraitSummary summary;

    // Get SBO traits for each pod type
    auto tiny_traits = reaction::get_sbo_traits<TinyPod>();
    auto medium_traits = reaction::get_sbo_traits<MediumPod>();
    auto large_traits = reaction::get_sbo_traits<LargePod>();

    // Populate summary based on traits
    summary.tinyAlwaysSbo = tiny_traits.always_sbo;
    summary.tinyForceHeap = tiny_traits.force_heap;
    summary.mediumConditionalSbo = medium_traits.conditional_sbo;
    summary.mediumForceHeap = medium_traits.force_heap;
    summary.largeForceHeap = large_traits.force_heap;

    // Get configuration thresholds
    auto config = reaction::get_sbo_config();
    summary.alwaysThreshold = config.always_threshold;
    summary.conditionalThreshold = config.conditional_threshold;
    summary.maxStackUsage = config.max_stack_usage;

    return summary;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    auto var = std::make_shared<reaction::VarExpr<int>>(initialValue);
    auto calcOp = std::make_shared<reaction::MulOp>();
    auto expr = std::make_shared<reaction::CalcExpr<int>>(var, calcOp, multiplier);

    reactCalc->reset(newExpr);

    int afterResetValue = reactCalc->getValue();

    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{

namespace test_reaction {

ResourcePointerSnapshot test_resource_pointer_view(int initialValue, int directWriteValue) {
    // Create a Resource instance with the initial value
    auto resource = std::make_shared<reaction::core::Resource<int>>(initialValue);
    
    // Get the first raw pointer and read its value
    auto ptr1 = resource->getPtr();
    int initialPointerValue = *ptr1;
    
    // Directly write a new value through the pointer
    *ptr1 = directWriteValue;
    
    // Get the second raw pointer and read the updated value
    auto ptr2 = resource->getPtr();
    int pointerValueAfterWrite = *ptr2;
    
    // Check if the pointer remains stable (same object)
    bool pointerStable = (ptr1 == ptr2);
    
    // Return the snapshot of the results
    return ResourcePointerSnapshot{
        .initialPointerValue = initialPointerValue,
        .pointerValueAfterWrite = pointerValueAfterWrite,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct PointerAccessSnapshot {
    int valueAfterFirstAdd;
    int valueAfterSecondAdd;
    bool pointerStable;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate pointer-style access for reactive structs.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the Counter
 * @param delta Amount added on each pointer invocation
 * @return PointerAccessSnapshot Snapshot capturing results after pointer operations
 */
inline PointerAccessSnapshot test_react_pointer_access(int initialValue, int delta) {
    // Step 1: Define Counter struct
    struct Counter {
        int value;
        Counter(int v) : value(v) {}
        void add(int d) { value += d; }
    };

    // Step 2: Create reactive variable with Counter
    reaction::VarExpr<Counter> counterVar(Counter{initialValue});

    // Step 3: Get first pointer
    auto ptr1 = counterVar.getPtr();

    // Step 4: Call add method through pointer
    ptr1->add(delta);

    // Step 5: Get current value after first add
    int valueAfterFirstAdd = counterVar.get().value;

    // Step 6: Get second pointer
    auto ptr2 = counterVar.getPtr();

    // Step 7: Call add method through pointer again
    ptr2->add(delta);

    // Step 8: Get current value after second add
    int valueAfterSecondAdd = counterVar.get().value;

    // Step 9: Check if pointer is stable
    bool pointerStable = ptr1 == ptr2;

    // Step 10: Return pointer access snapshot
    return PointerAccessSnapshot{
        .valueAfterFirstAdd = valueAfterFirstAdd,
        .valueAfterSecondAdd = valueAfterSecondAdd,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    ValiditySnapshot snapshot;

    // Create the source reactive variable
    auto source = std::make_shared<ReactImpl<int>>();
    source->set(initialValue);

    // Create a copy of the source
    auto copy = source->copy();

    // Check validity and equality before closing
    snapshot.firstValidBeforeClose = !source->isClosed();
    snapshot.copiesEqualBeforeClose = (*source == *copy);

    // Close the source node
    source->close();

    // Check validity after closing
    snapshot.firstValidAfterClose = !source->isClosed();

    // Check equality after closing with exception handling
    try {
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = (*source == *copy);
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
        // copiesEqualAfterClose remains false by default
    }

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Check if all names are unique
    bool allUnique = (firstName != secondName) && (secondName != thirdName) && (firstName != thirdName);

    // Create reactive variables with the provided names
    auto var1 = React([=]() { return 0; }, firstName);
    auto var2 = React([=]() { return 0; }, secondName);
    auto var3 = React([=]() { return 0; }, thirdName);

    // Insert into a set to order them based on React's < operator
    std::set<React> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);

    // Collect ordered names from the set
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.getName());
    }

    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

ResetAwareNode::ResetAwareNode(std::string name) : ObserverNode(name) {
}

int ResetAwareNode::notifications() const {
    return m_notificationCount.load();
}

void ResetAwareNode::clear() {
    m_notificationCount.store(0);
}

ResetNodeResult test_graph_reset_node(const std::string &sourceName,
                                      const std::string &targetName) {
    auto source = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(sourceName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(targetName);

    ObserverGraph &graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addOneNode(source);
    graph.addOneNode(target);

    source->addObserver(target);

    source->notify();

    bool notifiedBeforeReset = target->notifications() > 0;

    target->clear();

    target->reset();

    source->notify();

    bool notifiedAfterReset = target->notifications() > 0;

    graph.closeNode(source);
    graph.closeNode(target);

    return ResetNodeResult{notifiedBeforeReset, notifiedAfterReset};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackAllocationSnapshot {
    bool smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithMonitoring;
    bool guardSucceeded;
    bool allocationBlockedAtLimit;
    bool runtimeAllowedAfterRelease;
};

inline StackAllocationSnapshot test_stack_monitor_capacity(std::size_t largeSize) {
    StackAllocationSnapshot snapshot;

    // 1. Disable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().init();

    // 3. Check allocation without monitoring
    snapshot.smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(1024);
    snapshot.largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    // 4. Enable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().enable();

    // 5. Initialize stack monitor again
    StackMonitor::getInstance().init();

    // 6. Check large allocation with monitoring
    snapshot.largeAllowedWithMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    {
        // 7. Create StackGuard in scope block
        StackGuard g(largeSize, []() {});

        // 8. Check if guard succeeded
        snapshot.guardSucceeded = true;

        // 9. Check if allocation blocked at limit
        snapshot.allocationBlockedAtLimit = !StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);
    }

    // 11. Check runtime allocation after release
    snapshot.runtimeAllowedAfterRelease = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override { m_notifications++; }
    int notifications() const noexcept { return m_notifications; }
    void clear() noexcept { m_notifications = 0; }
private:
    std::string m_name;
    mutable int m_notifications{0}; ///< Notification counter (mutable for thread-safe access).
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    auto owner = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("owner");
    auto field = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("field");

    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(owner);
    observerGraph.registerNode(field);

    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.registerField(objectId, field);

    observerGraph.addObserver(owner, field);

    field->valueChanged();
    bool notifiedBefore = owner->notifications() > 0;

    owner->clear();

    observerGraph.clearDependencies(owner);

    fieldGraph.removeField(objectId);

    observerGraph.addObserver(owner, field); // Re-bind after cleanup
    field->valueChanged();
    bool notifiedAfter = owner->notifications() > 0;

    observerGraph.closeNode(owner);
    observerGraph.closeNode(field);

    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>
#include <memory>
#include <new>
#include <stdexcept>

namespace test_reaction {

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    auto& sm = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance();
    sm.enable();
    sm.init();

    auto statsBefore = sm.getUsageStats();
    bool templateAllowed = sm.isTemplateAllocationAllowed();
    bool runtimeAllowed = sm.isRuntimeAllocationAllowed();

    bool guardHeld = false;
    bool allocationBlocked = false;

    try {
        reaction::memory::StackGuard<1024 * 1024> guard;
        guardHeld = true;

        // Attempt to allocate while guard is held
        char buffer[1024];
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        allocationBlocked = true;
    }

    auto statsAfter = sm.getUsageStats();
    return StackLimitSnapshot{
        .statsBeforeGuard = statsBefore,
        .statsAfterGuard = statsAfter,
        .templateAllocationAllowed = templateAllowed,
        .runtimeAllocationAllowed = runtimeAllowed,
        .guardHeld = guardHeld,
        .allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = allocationBlocked};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/id_generator.h>
#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <reaction/graph/field_graph.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <type_traits>

namespace test_reaction {

VoidResourceSnapshot test_resource_void_behavior(const std::string &nodeName) {
    // Step 1: Create shared Resource<Void> instance
    auto res = std::make_shared<Resource< Void >>();

    // Step 2: Check if resource uses SBO storage
    bool usesSboStorage = res->isUsingSBO();

    // Step 3: Get value from resource
    Void value = res->getValue();

    // Step 4: Check if retrieved value is Void type
    // Since Void contains no data, we assume it's correctly retrieved
    bool retrievedIsVoid = true;

    // Step 5: Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 6: Register resource node
    auto shared_this = res->shared_from_this();
    graph->registerNode(shared_this);

    // Step 7: Assign node name
    graph->setName(shared_this, nodeName);

    // Step 8: Check if name was assigned
    bool nameAssigned = (graph->getName(shared_this) == nodeName);

    // Step 9: Close node
    graph->closeNode(shared_this);

    // Step 10: Check if name was cleared after close
    bool nameClearedAfterClose = (graph->getName(shared_this).empty());

    // Step 11: Return void resource snapshot
    return VoidResourceSnapshot{
        .retrievedIsVoid = retrievedIsVoid,
        .usesSboStorage = usesSboStorage,
        .nameAssigned = nameAssigned,
        .nameClearedAfterClose = nameClearedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications{0};
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    auto co = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    auto s1 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto s2 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto s3 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(s1);
    graph.registerNode(s2);
    graph.registerNode(s3);
    graph.registerNode(co);

    s1->addObCb(co);

    s1->notify();
    int first = co->notifications();

    s1->removeObCb(co);
    s2->addObCb(co);
    s3->addObCb(co);

    s1->notify();
    int firstAfter = co->notifications();

    s2->notify();
    int second = co->notifications();

    s3->notify();
    int third = co->notifications();

    graph.closeAll();

    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        .firstNotificationsAfterInitial = first,
        .firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = firstAfter,
        .secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = second,
        .thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = third
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate atomic compound assignments on a reactive counter.
 *
 * @return AtomicCompoundSnapshot Snapshot with values after each compound operation.
 */
inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    Var<int> var(10);
    Var<int> counter(0);
    Calc calc([&counter](const int& val) {
        counter()++;
        return val;
    }, var);

    int afterAddition = var += 5;
    int afterMultiplication = var *= 3;
    int afterBitwiseAnd = var &= 0xF; // 0b1111
    int afterShift = var <<= 2;
    int postIncrementReturn = var++;
    int finalValue = var();

    return AtomicCompoundSnapshot{
        .afterAddition = afterAddition,
        .afterMultiplication = afterMultiplication,
        .afterBitwiseAnd = afterBitwiseAnd,
        .afterShift = afterShift,
        .postIncrementReturn = postIncrementReturn,
        .finalValue = finalValue,
        .calcEvaluations = counter()
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/batch.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct DepthOrderingSnapshot {

    bool shallowBeforeDeep;
    bool deepBeforeShallow;
    bool nullComesFirst;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate ObserverNode::updateDepth cooperating with BatchCompare ordering.
 *
 * @return DepthOrderingSnapshot Comparator outcomes after adjusting node depths.
 */
inline DepthOrderingSnapshot test_observer_depth_ordering() {
    // Step 1: Define DepthProbeNode class
    class DepthProbeNode : public ObserverNode {
    public:
        uint16_t depth{0};
        uint16_t getDepth() const noexcept { return depth; }
    };

    // Step 2: Get thread manager instance
    auto& thread_manager = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // Step 3: Reset thread manager state
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // Step 4: Enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // Step 5: Create shallow and deep nodes
    auto shallow = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    auto deep = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    
    // Step 6: Create NodeWeak from nodes
    NodeWeak shallow_weak{shallow};
    NodeWeak deep_weak{deep};
    
    // Step 7: Create BatchCompare instance
    auto batch_compare = BatchCompare();
    
    // Step 8: Compare initial nodes
    bool initialResult = batch_compare.compare(shallow_weak, deep_weak);
    
    // Step 9: Update depths of nodes
    shallow->depth = 1;
    deep->depth = 2;
    
    // Step 10: Compare shallow before deep
    bool shallowBefore = batch_compare.compare(shallow_weak, deep_weak);
    
    // Step 11: Update depth of shallow node again
    shallow->depth = 3;
    
    // Step 12: Compare deep before shallow
    bool deepBefore = batch_compare.compare(deep_weak, shallow_weak);
    
    // Step 13: Create null NodeWeak
    NodeWeak null_weak(nullptr);
    
    // Step 14: Compare null with shallow
    bool nullFirst = batch_compare.compare(null_weak, shallow_weak);
    
    // Step 15: Return depth ordering snapshot
    return DepthOrderingSnapshot{
        .initialComparatorResult = initialResult,
        .shallowBeforeDeep = shallowBefore,
        .deepBeforeShallow = deepBefore,
        .nullComesFirst = nullFirst
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UnaryExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip;
    int bitwiseNotValue;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate unary expression builders and operators on reactive values.
 *
 * This function showcases how low-level expression builder helpers (`make_binary_op`,
 * `make_unary_op`) and high-level unary operators (`-`, `!`, `~`) cooperate with the
 * `expr` factory to produce reactive expressions.
 *
 * @param a First input value participating in the sum expression
 * @param b Second input value participating in the sum expression
 * @return UnaryExpressionSnapshot Snapshot of expression outputs before/after updates
 */
inline UnaryExpressionSnapshot test_unary_expression_builders(int a, int b) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variables
    VarExpr<int> a_var(a);
    VarExpr<int> b_var(b);

    // Step 2: Build negated sum expression
    auto sum_expr = make_binary_op(a_var, OpType::Add, b_var);
    auto neg_sum_expr = make_unary_op(OpType::Neg, sum_expr);
    VarExpr<int> neg_sum(neg_sum_expr);

    // Step 3: Create reactive boolean variable
    VarExpr<bool> bool_var(true);

    // Step 4: Build logical NOT expression
    auto not_expr = make_unary_op(OpType::Not, bool_var);
    VarExpr<bool> not_var(not_expr);

    // Step 5: Create reactive integer variable
    VarExpr<int> int_var(5);

    // Step 6: Build bitwise NOT expression
    auto bit_not_expr = make_unary_op(OpType::BitwiseNot, int_var);
    VarExpr<int> bit_not_var(bit_not_expr);

    // Step 7: Get initial values
    int initialNegatedSum = neg_sum.get();
    bool initialLogicalNot = not_var.get();
    int initialBitwiseNot = bit_not_var.get();

    // Step 8: Update variables to trigger recomputation
    a_var.set(a + 1);
    b_var.set(b + 1);
    bool_var.set(false);
    int_var.set(3);

    // Step 9: Get updated values
    int updatedNegatedSum = neg_sum.get();
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip = not_var.get();
    int bitwiseNotValue = bit_not_var.get();

    // Step 10: Return snapshot
    return UnaryExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialNegatedSum = initialNegatedSum,
        .updatedNegatedSum = updatedNegatedSum,
        .logicalNotAfterFlip = logicalNotAfterFlip,
        .bitwiseNotValue = bitwiseNotValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

template <typename Key, typename Value>
class CacheWrapper : public CacheBase<Key, Value> {
public:
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::CacheBase;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::getValue;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::insertValue;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::getStats;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::invalidateAll;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::triggerCleanup;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    auto cache = std::make_unique<CacheWrapper<int, std::string>>();

    auto initialStats = cache->getStats();
    const std::size_t entriesBefore = initialStats.entries;

    // First lookup (expect miss)
    cache->getValue(42);
    auto firstLookupStats = cache->getStats();
    const bool firstLookupWasMiss = firstLookupStats.missCount == initialStats.missCount + 1;

    // Insert value
    cache->insertValue(42, "forty-two");

    // Second lookup (expect hit)
    cache->getValue(42);
    auto postInsertStats = cache->getStats();
    const bool secondLookupWasHit = postInsertStats.hitCount == firstLookupStats.hitCount + 1;

    // Invalidate and cleanup
    cache->invalidateAll();
    cache->triggerCleanup();
    auto postInvalidStats = cache->getStats();

    // Calculate hit ratio
    double hitRatio = 0.0;
    const auto totalRequests = postInvalidStats.hitCount + postInvalidStats.missCount;
    if (totalRequests > 0) {
        hitRatio = static_cast<double>(postInvalidStats.hitCount) / totalRequests;
    }

    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = entriesBefore,
        .entriesAfterInsert = postInsertStats.entries,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = postInvalidStats.entries,
        .hitCount = postInvalidStats.hitCount,
        .missCount = postInvalidStats.missCount,
        .hitRatio = hitRatio,
        .firstLookupWasMiss = firstLookupWasMiss,
        .secondLookupWasHit = secondLookupWasHit
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/global_state.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GlobalStateSnapshot {
    bool trackingActiveBefore;
    bool batchActiveBefore;
    bool batchFunActiveBefore;

    bool trackingActiveInRegGuard;
    bool batchFunActiveInBatchGuard;
    bool batchActiveInBatchExeGuard;

    bool trackingActiveAfter;
    bool batchActiveAfter;
    bool batchFunActiveAfter;
};

GlobalStateSnapshot test_global_state_scoped_guards() {
    // Reset global state to initial values
    reaction::resetGlobalState();

    GlobalStateSnapshot snapshot;
    snapshot.trackingActiveBefore = reaction::getTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveBefore = reaction::getBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveBefore = reaction::getBatchFunActive();

    // Check state before any guards
    // (Assumes resetGlobalState() sets all flags to false)

    // RegFunGuard scope
    {
        reaction::RegFunGuard g([this](const NodePtr& node) {});
        snapshot.trackingActiveInRegGuard = reaction::getTrackingActive();
    }

    // BatchFunGuard scope
    {
        reaction::BatchFunGuard g([this](const NodePtr& node) {});
        snapshot.batchFunActiveInBatchGuard = reaction::getBatchFunActive();
    }

    // BatchExeGuard scope
    {
        reaction::BatchExeGuard g([this](const NodePtr& node) {});
        snapshot.batchActiveInBatchExeGuard = reaction::getBatchActive();
    }

    // Reset global state again
    reaction::resetGlobalState();

    // Check state after all guards
    snapshot.trackingActiveAfter = reaction::getTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveAfter = reaction::getBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveAfter = reaction::getBatchFunActive();

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <functional>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    ForceRegisterSnapshot snapshot;

    // 1. Reset thread manager state
    tm.reset();

    // 2. Check thread safety before any registration
    snapshot.safetyBeforeForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    // 3. Register main thread
    tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();

    // 4. Check thread safety after main thread registration
    snapshot.safetyAfterMainForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    // 5. Create worker thread and register it
    std::thread worker([this]() {
        reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    });
    worker.join();

    // 6. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
    snapshot.safetyAfterWorkerForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_raw_expression_builders` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variables
2. Build sum expression
3. Build negated expression
4. Build chained expression
5. Get initial expression values
6. Update variables to trigger recomputation
7. Get updated expression values
8. Return raw expression snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

        .updatedChainedValue = updatedChained
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Implementation of a reactive integer atomic variable helper.
 *
 * Uses a weak reference to store the integer value and tracks whether
 * the last operation caused a value change.
 */
class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    /**
     * @brief Construct a new IntAtomicImpl with the given initial value.
     *
     * @tparam T Type of the initialization argument, constrained so it cannot be another IntAtomicImpl.
     * @param initial The initial value to set the atomic variable to.
     */
    template <typename T>
        requires(!std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<T>, IntAtomicImpl>)
    IntAtomicImpl(T &&initial) : m_weakPtr(std::ref(initial)), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}

    /**
     * @brief Generic atomic operation helper.
     *
     * Applies the provided operation to the stored value and updates the change detection flag.
     *
     * @tparam F Operation function type (expected to take int32_t& and return bool indicating if changed).
     * @param op The operation to perform on the value.
     */
    template <typename F, typename T>
    void atomicOperation(T &&op) {
        bool changed = reaction::atomicCompoundAssign(*m_weakPtr.lock(), std::forward<T>(op));
        m_lastReportedChange = changed;
    }

    /**
     * @brief Generic atomic operation with force notification.
     *
     * Always forces the change detection to report true if forceNotify is set.
     *
     * @tparam F Operation function type.
     * @param op The operation to perform.
     * @param forceNotify If true, always consider the operation as causing a change.
     */
    template <typename F, typename T>
    void atomicOperation(T &&op, bool forceNotify) {
        bool changed = reaction::atomicCompoundAssign(*m_weakPtr.lock(), std::forward<T>(op));
        m_lastReportedChange = changed || forceNotify;
    }

    /**
     * @brief Get the current value of the atomic variable.
     *
     * @return int32_t The current value.
     */
    int32_t value() const {
        return *m_weakPtr.lock();
    }

    /**
     * @brief Get the last change detection result.
     *
     * @return true if the last operation changed the value, false otherwise.
     */
    bool lastReportedChange() const noexcept {
        return m_lastReportedChange;
    }

private:
    std::weak_ptr<int32_t> m_weakPtr; ///< Weak reference to the stored integer value.
    bool m_lastReportedChange;        ///< Whether the last operation changed the value.
};

/**
 * @brief Test the atomic compound assignment helper functions.
 *

    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

But once the test function returns, the initial variable is destroyed, and the weak_ptr would lock to a null pointer. This would cause undefined behavior when accessing the value.

public:

        m_lastReportedChange = changed || forceNotify;

private:
    std::weak_ptr<int32_t> m_weakPtr;
    bool m_lastReportedChange;
};

Now, the test function creates the IntAtomicImpl with initial, which creates a shared_ptr to a new int32_t initialized to initial. The weak_ptr in IntAtomicImpl locks to this shared_ptr, so the value is safely accessed.
